title: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia
wikitext:
[]: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by C. Smith » Tue Dec 18, 2018 10:08 pm According to her Wikipedia page, Rei is categorized as having Schizophrenia. Additionally, Anno has admitted in an interview that he tried to make her a schizophrenic but couldn't. But let's see if Rei really has it, based on the DSM-V criteria (as outlined below). SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Bhorium » Wed Dec 19, 2018 8:05 am The premise of slapping a human diagnosis on someone who is at least somewhat beyond human is questionable.

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 21, 2018 9:09 am The Wikipedia claim is unsourced, so forget that. If the interview statement does exist, I'm curious where it's from so I can look at the original wording.

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Shamsiel-kun » Fri Dec 21, 2018 1:48 pm My theory is that Rei is actually a heavy drinker of medicinal alcohol behind the scenes, who is very good at hiding it. Eva is us witnessing her Delirium tremens increasing from moderately benign to completely bonkers. Besides, the exact nature of the Rei-quarium fluid is not known to us. TL;DR version: people should not try to diagnose animated characters (or long dead people for that matter) with <insert popular/favourite DSM disorder>. We had the "Rei has assburgers /autism"-fad already in the past...

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Nerd King 101 » Sun Jan 20, 2019 6:00 pm Rei Ayanami is not human and was raised by Gendo and is technically like 4 when the series starts. I think her issues stem from that

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 20, 2019 8:25 pm "Not human" isn't accurate and tells us nothing; the series itself is clear on her humanity. I think her problems can be mostly attributed to a combination of (A) being an ancient alien in the wrong body whose soul has been artificially fragmented, and (B) being intentionally raised in a manipulative and deprivatory manner.

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Ambiant23 » Sun Jan 20, 2019 10:36 pm I remember the Rei has autism thing. Also Shinji is listed on the wikipedia for fictional bisexual characters

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Melkor » Sun Jan 20, 2019 11:09 pm Ambiant23 wrote: I remember the Rei has autism thing.

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Blockio » Sat Jan 26, 2019 6:02 pm Melkor wrote: What was the consensus on that? This is the first I've heard of it, but now that I think about it she does seem to display behaviors of someone with autism.

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by C. Smith » Sat Feb 02, 2019 10:37 pm Blockio wrote: Doesn't really hold up to scrutiny, the parallels are only surface level http://forum.evageeks.org/post/871305/Ayanami-and-Autism/#871305

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by ShonHam » Sun Feb 03, 2019 5:47 am Rei's behaviour is a bi-product of her upbringing I believe. If Anno wished to make her schizophrenic, then it seems it remained a concept and nothing more.

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by C. Smith » Mon Feb 04, 2019 3:01 am ShonHam wrote: Rei's behaviour is a bi-product of her upbringing I believe. If Anno wished to make her schizophrenic, then it seems it remained a concept and nothing more.

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Feb 04, 2019 11:37 am Second request -- any chance we can get a source on Anno's "intent" here?

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun Feb 10, 2019 5:50 am Rei Ayanami has loveleiness.

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Amalgam429 » Sun Feb 10, 2019 10:39 am I'm with the masses on this one. Rei is another persons soul, combined with a god, inside a lillin vessel. I think it's very unfair to judge her state of mind based on human science. Whatever issues she may have probably relate to state of being rather than anything medical. Furthermore, if she did have some sort of condition like that, I'm sure Ritsuko or Gendo would've taken note and acted accordingly. The fact that they never focus on any of those symptoms throughout the course of either her arcs or just the main series in general makes me think a medical condition like that was not supposed to be suggested in the airing of the show

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Berserkelion » Sun Jul 12, 2020 10:44 am Reichu wrote: The Wikipedia claim is unsourced, so forget that. If the interview statement does exist, I'm curious where it's from so I can look at the original wording.

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by orcot » Sun Jul 12, 2020 12:21 pm Could it be mistranslated from schizoid? SPD could be a potential fit Furthermore, if she did have some sort of condition like that, I'm sure Ritsuko or Gendo would've taken note and acted accordingly.

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by Berserkelion » Mon Jul 13, 2020 7:24 pm orcot wrote: Could it be mistranslated from schizoid?

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Wed Jul 22, 2020 8:44 pm Reichu wrote: The Wikipedia claim is unsourced, so forget that. If the interview statement does exist, I'm curious where it's from so I can look at the original wording.

[]: Re: Does Rei Ayanami have schizophrenia?             Quote Post by ErgoProxy » Thu Jul 23, 2020 8:31 pm Anno Hideaki wrote: I had intended to make Rei a schizophrenic (分裂症的) character.
